notes: Editor polish + fixes. The page editor's properties panel no longer has an 'Apply Changes' button — every edit (text, color, size, weight, alignment, spacing, links, anchors, image source/alt) now applies automatically as you make it. Text alignment now applies reliably (written with !important so a theme's own rules can't override it). Buttons and links gain Hover controls — set a hover background and text color, saved as a per-element :hover rule in the page. Per-page heading styles now cover H1–H6 (was H1–H3) and add a Google Fonts picker; the chosen fonts are loaded on the page automatically. New pages and the privacy-policy page now sit in a centered content section with real side margins and room below the header (no more overlap), while still pulling in the site's real header and footer. Fixed the Restore button in Site Foundry → Manage Pages (it wasn't firing due to a quote-escaping bug) and removed the now-redundant remove buttons there. Reworded client-facing messages so they no longer reference internal system files. Previous (1.13.3) — Subtle version stamp in the bottom-left of the sidebar (small gray monospace, e.g. 'v1.13.3') so you can see which build is loaded at a glance — it reads the live CMS_VERSION and hides when the sidebar is collapsed. Previous (1.13.2) — New + generated pages now inherit the site header/footer. The privacy-policy generator and the New Page action used to emit a standalone document with no header/nav/footer; they now clone a real page (its head/CSS + header + nav + footer) and swap in the new content, so created pages match the rest of the site (falls back to a standalone doc only if there's no donor page). Resize anchoring fixed properly: a normal drag now keeps the OPPOSITE edge/corner fixed (drag the bottom-right and the left/top stay put) no matter how the element is centered — it probes how margin maps to on-screen position, then measures and corrects so the anchor holds even for flex- or text-align-centered elements (the old margin-pin didn't work on those, so it looked symmetric). Hold Cmd (Mac) / Ctrl (PC) for symmetric resize from the center. Site Foundry → Manage Pages: removed the redundant 'Remove from CMS' buttons (removal lives in the editor's gear → Page Settings); the page list + Restore for hidden pages remain. The sidebar 'Pages' count (and dashboard stat) now update immediately whenever a page is removed, deleted, restored, or added. Design tab: brand colors are now Primary + Secondary, bound to the --brand-color-primary / --brand-color-secondary CSS variables. They auto-fill from the site's CSS (and empty Contact/Social fields auto-fill from the site's links); 'Apply colors to all pages' injects a managed :root override into every page so the variables update site-wide. Previous (1.13.1) — Fixes. Remove-from-CMS (Site Foundry → Manage Pages, and the Page Settings gear): now matches the page by its derived id (pageIdFromPath normalizes the path deterministically) plus a normalized full-path backstop, instead of brittle filename/path string compares — so removal sticks for subdirectory pages and survives path-format drift (case, leading ./ or /). It no longer wrongly hides a different page that happens to share a filename (e.g. services/plumbing.html vs plumbing.html), and loadPages now makes sure site.json is loaded before filtering so a removed page can't reappear when the editor opens before the Design tab. Div/container resize handles: each drag now sets an absolute size from a fresh snapshot and the element no longer re-centers between drags — margin-left/top are pinned on the active axis so an auto-centered element (margin:0 auto) stays put. Default drag anchors the opposite edge/corner; hold Cmd (Mac) / Ctrl (PC) to resize symmetrically from the center (opposite edge moves equally), like Photoshop. Previous (1.13.0) — Password-protect pages (PHP session gate). Page Settings now has a 'Password-protect this page' toggle + password field. When enabled, api.php stores a bcrypt hash of the password in admin/protect.secret.php (a PHP file, never served as source), (re)writes a public _fourge_gate.php, and maintains a managed RewriteRule block in the root .htaccess so the page routes through the gate. Visitors get a branded password screen; on the correct password the gate sets a PHP session cookie and serves the page (path-traversal-safe, only pages in the protect list are servable, wrong attempts are slowed and the hash never reaches the browser). Protected pages show a lock icon in the editor's page list. NOTE: requires the updated api.php deployed by hand (api.php is always a manual deploy); the gate file + .htaccess rules are written automatically the first time you protect a page. Previous (1.12.0) — Responsive editing (Elementor-style funnel) + reverse columns. The Desktop/Tablet/Mobile toggle in the editor now scopes your edits: switch to Tablet or Mobile and any style change (font size, color, weight, alignment, spacing, radius, background, width/height — including drag-resize) is written as a per-device media-query rule instead of an inline style, keyed to a stable per-element class. Values funnel down the cascade — Desktop applies everywhere, Tablet overrides tablet+mobile (≤1024px), Mobile overrides mobile (≤600px) — so you only override what differs. The properties panel shows a banner telling you which device you're editing. Flex containers get a Direction control (row / row-reverse / column / column-reverse); set it per device to reverse or stack columns on phones. Overrides are saved into a managed <style id=fourge-responsive> block in the page and the element classes, so they reconstruct correctly when you reopen the page (and a new override never wipes existing ones). Previous (1.11.0) — Collapsible side menus + drag-to-resize containers. The Desktop/Tablet/Mobile toggle in the editor now scopes your edits: switch to Tablet or Mobile and any style change (font size, color, weight, alignment, spacing, radius, background, width/height — including drag-resize) is written as a per-device media-query rule instead of an inline style, keyed to a stable per-element class. Values funnel down the cascade — Desktop applies everywhere, Tablet overrides tablet+mobile (≤1024px), Mobile overrides mobile (≤600px) — so you only override what differs. The properties panel shows a banner telling you which device you're editing. Flex containers get a Direction control (row / row-reverse / column / column-reverse); set it per device to reverse or stack columns on phones. Overrides are saved into a managed <style id=fourge-responsive> block in the page and the element classes, so they reconstruct correctly when you reopen the page (and a new override never wipes existing ones). Previous (1.11.0) — Collapsible side menus + drag-to-resize containers. Both side menus now collapse: a toggle by the logo collapses the main sidebar, and a toggle in the editor toolbar collapses the page list in the page editor — each slides to zero width and the choice persists per browser. Drag-to-resize: selecting a container that holds text (or an image) in the visual editor now shows eight resize handles (four corners + four edge midpoints). Drag any handle to resize — corners resize both dimensions, edges resize one; dragging the left/top edges keeps the opposite edge anchored (margin compensation). The new width/height (and any margin) are written as inline styles on the element, so the change is saved accurately into the page's HTML. A resize is one undo step. Previous (1.10.0) — Editor + site polish from the testing meeting. Links: the properties panel now has an 'Open in a new tab' checkbox for any link (sets target=_blank + rel=noopener noreferrer) and clears it cleanly when unchecked. Anchors: every element (especially sections and columns) now has an editable 'Anchor ID' field, so you can link to a spot on the page with #id — the id is sanitized to a valid anchor as you type, and the panel shows the exact #link to use. Favicon: Design → Design now has a Favicon uploader (square PNG/SVG/ICO) with a live preview; 'Apply to all pages' injects a managed <link rel=icon> (+ apple-touch-icon) into every page and Remove clears it. Per-page heading styles: Page Settings now has H1/H2/H3 overrides (size, weight, alignment, color) that compile to scoped, !important CSS injected only into that page on Save, so a page can override the site design without touching global styles. Go Live tool: Settings now has a 'Find & Replace URL' card (Architect only) to swap a staging/preview domain for the production domain across all pages, posts, sitemap.xml and robots.txt — with a non-destructive Preview (per-file occurrence counts) before applying, and it also fixes the website URL in site.json. Renamed the last visible 'SITE FACTORY' label to 'SITE FOUNDRY'. Previous (1.9.0) — Page management: each page in the editor now has a gear → Page Settings (Custom Page CSS + Custom Page JavaScript injected into that page only on save, Draft toggle that noindexes + drops the page from the sitemap, Delete page, and Remove-from-CMS). Targeted-landing pages now show a red dot in the page list. Fixed Remove-from-CMS not sticking (the hidden-page match now checks path OR file OR id). Polish: the SEO Generate buttons now read 'Generate' / 'Generate All' (dropped 'with AI') with a clean Font Awesome icon instead of the sparkle emoji — no emojis in the UI. Renamed the Site Factory module to Site Foundry throughout. SEO module rebuilt (Rank Math-style): AI generation now reads each page's REAL content (fixes the 'Page Not Found' metadata bug where it read empty CMS blocks on scanned pages) and writes context-aware, business/location-specific titles + descriptions. Per-page SEO now includes focus keyword with a live on-page analysis checklist + score, canonical URL, robots index/noindex + follow/nofollow, Open Graph + Twitter card fields, a Google snippet preview, and a per-page schema type. New 'Apply to all pages' writes managed meta tags + JSON-LD (site-wide LocalBusiness/Organization + per-page schema) into every page file and regenerates sitemap.xml + robots.txt. Sitemap generator and robots.txt editor added. (Redirections + a 404 monitor are the next SEO addition.) Also fixed the editor toolbar: the Visual/Code toggle is centered and the device buttons no longer break when returning from Code view. Code editor for the page editor: a Visual ⇄ Code toggle in the editor toolbar opens a VS Code-style source view (CodeMirror, syntax-highlighted HTML + inline CSS/JS, dark theme, line numbers, auto-close tags, Ctrl/Cmd+S to save). Switching back to Visual ingests any hand-edits so they stay editable visually; saving in Code mode writes the source directly. Fixed a media-gallery bug where, on a filtered view (e.g. Videos), clicking a tile opened/copied the item at that slot in the unfiltered 'All' list instead of the one you clicked. Editor preview now fills the canvas: the desktop preview is the largest 16:9 box that fits (rendering at a real desktop width — at least 1440, larger on big monitors) plus a Fill-screen button (native fullscreen). Fixed self profile editing — the signed-in user's id and email are now stored in the session so the Account modal populates and saves (was 'Please sign in again'). Added custom code injection points in Analytics: Functional/Analytics/Marketing each with a header and a body field, injected into <head> and before </body> via the existing Apply-to-All-Pages sweep. Account/Settings cleanup: removed the Updates card and the Mailgun card from Settings (Mailgun is now configured server-side in config.secret.php so the API key never passes through the browser); removed the top-right notification bell; the top-right avatar is now a profile menu (Account / Change password / Sign out) and the change-password + sign-out buttons moved out of the bottom-left into it; signed-in users can now edit their own profile (name + email) via the new Account modal. ADAptify panel isolation fix: the widget is now appended to <html> (a sibling of <body>) and every visitor effect is scoped to <body>, so the panel itself can never be recolored, filtered, zoomed, or resized by the page — verified the menu stays readable under High Contrast/Invert. ADAptify full option set: the widget now offers Readable Font, Highlight Titles/Links, Text Magnifier, Content Scaling + Font Size + Line Height + Letter Spacing steppers (±10% per click), text alignment, Dark/Light/High contrast, Monochrome, Invert, High/Low saturation, custom text/title/background colors, Mute Sounds, Hide Images, Reading Guide, Reading Mask, Stop Animations, Highlight on Hover/Click, and Large Cursor — grouped into Content/Color/Navigation sections, all persisted per visitor. Updates are now ALWAYS automatic: the manual update card is removed and every site installs the latest engine at login, so no one can run a stale build (re-introduce a gate before a major release if needed). Security: enforce HTTPS for credentials. A new root .htaccess redirects all HTTP traffic to HTTPS site-wide (proxy-aware to avoid redirect loops), and api.php now refuses sign-in / password / secret actions over plain HTTP as a server-side backstop (localhost exempt; toggle with 'require_https' in config.secret.php). Passwords were already hashed at rest with Argon2id/bcrypt; this closes the in-transit gap. Hotfix (1.4.1): neutralized a literal closing-script sequence in an ADAptify comment that truncated admin/index.html and broke login. ADAptify — a site-wide accessibility widget (contrast, text size, grayscale, invert, readable font, highlight links, big cursor, pause motion) with its own CMS tab. Enable/disable, placement, and brand colors are stored server-side in data/adaptify.json and apply to every page instantly. One-click install drops the widget script across all pages. Plus the 1.3.0 work: server-level page list, automatic server connection, server-side settings, Revisions ledger, faithful HTML import, WAF-proof multipart save, square media picker + 16:9 desktop editor, FAQ/accordion editing, and Privacy Policy generator.
apiNote: admin/api.php holds this site's secrets (API token) and is never auto-updated. If a release changes api.php, update it by hand. 1.13.0 adds the 'set_page_password' action (per-page password protection): deploy the updated api.php by hand for page protection to work — the gate file (_fourge_gate.php) and .htaccess rules are written automatically when you first protect a page; the bcrypt hashes live in admin/protect.secret.php (never served as source). 1.4.2 adds HTTPS enforcement to api.php (the credential-action backstop) — deploy the updated api.php and the new root .htaccess by hand to get the full effect; the redirect and guard only protect a site once those two files are in place. 1.4.0 adds 'adaptify.js' to api.php's repo-fetch allow-list — only needed for installing the widget from a PRIVATE repo.
